messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following CCTV surveillance policy for GDPR compliance:

"CCTV cameras are in operation on these premises for security purposes. Recording is continuous 24/7. Footage is retained for 30 days and then overwritten. Access to footage is restricted to security personnel and management. Footage may be shared with law enforcement on request."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Security purpose implies legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)), but no explicit lawful basis is stated and no legitimate interest assessment is referenced.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] CCTV footage can capture biometric data and reveal racial or ethnic origin. No acknowledgement or safeguard for incidental special category data capture is included.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] "Security purposes" is stated but vague. Sharing with law enforcement introduces a secondary purpose not separately justified.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Continuous 24/7 recording may be disproportionate. No explanation of why blanket recording is necessary.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] "30 days and then overwritten" is a clearly defined and reasonable retention period.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] No mention of data subject rights. Individuals captured on CCTV have the right to access footage of themselves (Art. 15) and to object (Art. 21).

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Law enforcement sharing is mentioned but no managed security or CCTV monitoring provider processing footage as a data processor is mentioned.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. If cloud storage or remote monitoring services are used, transfer implications are unaddressed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not mentioned. Large-scale systematic monitoring may require a DPO and a DPIA under Art. 35.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not stated), Art. 9 (biometric/special category risk unaddressed), Art. 5 (vague purpose, proportionality not justified), Art. 15-21 (no data subject rights), Art. 37 (DPO and DPIA not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State legitimate interest as lawful basis and reference a documented LIA
- Acknowledge risk of capturing special category data and state safeguards
- Clarify scope of monitoring and justify continuous recording as proportionate
- Add data subject rights section including how individuals can request access to footage
- Disclose any third-party CCTV management processors
- Conduct and reference a DPIA given systematic monitoring
- Add breach notification commitment
